title: Days of Inventory Cover Calculator
meta_description: Calculate how many days your current stock will last at average demand. Enter available stock and average daily demand to get days of cover.

inputs.label: Available stock
inputs.help: Current on-hand quantity (or value, if demand is in the same unit).

inputs.label: Average daily demand
inputs.help: Average units sold or consumed per day.

result_cards.label: Days of cover
result_cards.description: How many days current stock lasts at average usage.

copy_blocks.intro: Days of inventory cover estimates how long your current stock will last at the average usage rate. It is a fast health check for each item and for total inventory.
copy_blocks.how_it_works: Divide available stock by average daily demand. Keep both in the same unit, whether units, cases, or value.
copy_blocks.formula_explanation: Days of cover = available stock / average daily demand. For a value basis over a year, weeks of cover = stock value / annual usage x 52.
copy_blocks.worked_example: With 600 units on hand and average demand of 100 units/day, days of cover = 600 / 100 = 6 days.

faq.q: What is the difference from inventory turnover?
faq.a: Days of cover is forward-looking from current stock and expected demand; turnover is a backward-looking ratio over a completed period.

faq.q: Does it account for demand spikes?
faq.a: No. It uses average demand, so pair it with safety stock and a reorder point for variable items.

faq.q: Can I use it on value instead of units?
faq.a: Yes, as long as stock and demand are both expressed in the same unit or currency basis.
